name: Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite
name_original: Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitológica de El Planerón — ida y vuelta desde Belchite
region_intro: Touring cycling around Belchite offers routes through a distinctive landscape characterized by arid desert sceneries and historical sites. The region features river oases that provide a contrast to the dry environment, alongside significant Roman engineering landmarks. Cyclists can explore areas like the Birds Reserve 'El Planerón' and the poignant ruins of the Old Village of Belchite. The terrain includes a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents, suitable for various touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Belchite The most popular touring cycling route is Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic Old Village and leads to the El Planerón Birds Reserve. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Rodén and Salada de Mediana Loop, a difficult 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This trail features varied terrain with an elevation gain of 893 feet (272 meters). Local touring cyclists also love the Museum of the Mummies and the Quinto Pickets — gravel circular from Quinto via the Natural Path of the Ebro, a 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail leading through the Natural Path of the Ebro, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Belchite is defined by desert sceneries, river oases, and historical ruins, including sites of Roman engineering. The network offers options for moderate to difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Belchite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Belchite?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Belchite, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes explore the region's unique desert landscapes, historical sites, and natural features.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Belchite?
A: The terrain around Belchite is characterized by striking desert sceneries, with a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents. You'll also find refreshing river oases that contrast with the arid environment. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993395116/roden-and-salada-de-mediana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodén and Salada de Mediana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, feature varied terrain with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy touring cycling routes in Belchite?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region does offer paths suitable for various abilities. For example, the Almonacid de la Cuba-Belchite Trail is an easy 6.73 km trail mentioned in regional research, allowing exploration of Roman engineering and the Old Village. For specific komoot routes, most listed are moderate, so checking individual route details for distance and elevation is recommended for families.

Q: What historical sites can I visit while touring cycling in Belchite?
A: Belchite is rich in history. Many routes incorporate the poignant ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, a 'ghost town' preserved from the Spanish Civil War. You can also find sites of Roman engineering, such as the Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994091194/roman-dam-of-almonacid-letux-azuara-circular-from-almonacid-de-la-cuba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid - Letux - Azuara circular from Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any natural features or reserves to explore by bike?
A: Yes, the region offers unique natural attractions. You can cycle to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Planerón Ornithological Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, a protected area for birds, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salada de Mediana Salt Flat&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de los Chorros&lt;/a&gt; are other notable natural monuments in the area.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for touring cycling routes in Belchite?
A: Touring cycling routes in Belchite vary in length and elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; is about 30.8 km (19.1 miles) with around 200 meters (650 feet) of elevation gain. A more challenging option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Dirt Road Through Olive Groves loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, covers approximately 68 km (42 miles) with over 740 meters (2400 feet) of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Belchite?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994102639/museum-of-the-mummies-and-the-quinto-pickets-gravel-circular-from-quinto-via-the-natural-path-of-the-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Museum of the Mummies and the Quinto Pickets — gravel circular from Quinto via the Natural Path of the Ebro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Belchite?
A: Belchite is situated in one of the driest areas of Aragón, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. These seasons offer milder temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter, providing a more comfortable experience for exploring the arid landscapes.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Belchite?
A: Belchite, including the new town, offers various parking options. When planning your route, consider starting points within or near the town center, or at specific trailheads, where parking facilities are typically available. For routes starting from nearby villages like Quinto or Almonacid de la Cuba, parking can usually be found in the respective village centers.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Belchite?
A: Touring cyclists often praise Belchite for its distinctive desert sceneries, the historical depth offered by the Old Village, and the contrast provided by river oases. The region's mix of moderate to difficult routes caters to different ability levels, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with cultural immersion is highly valued.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat along the touring cycling routes?
A: The new town of Belchite features amenities like cafes and restaurants, particularly around 'Plaza España.' For longer routes that pass through other villages, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994091194/roman-dam-of-almonacid-letux-azuara-circular-from-almonacid-de-la-cuba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid - Letux - Azuara circular from Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;, you may find local establishments in those settlements to refuel.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Belchite?
A: Belchite is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger cities like Zaragoza. While direct access to specific trailheads might require some cycling from the bus stop, public transport can be a viable option for reaching the general area and starting your touring cycling adventure.
